member.name: T:Jint.Collections.DictionarySlim`2
member.summary: DictionarySlim<string, TValue> is similar to Dictionary<TKey, TValue> but optimized in three ways: 1) It allows access to the value by ref replacing the common TryGetValue and Add pattern. 2) It does not store the hash code (assumes it is cheap to equate values). 3) It does not accept an equality comparer (assumes Object.GetHashCode() and Object.Equals() or overridden implementation are cheap and sufficient).

member.name: M:Jint.Collections.DictionarySlim`2.Clear
member.summary: Clears the dictionary. Note that this invalidates any active enumerators.

member.name: M:Jint.Collections.DictionarySlim`2.GetOrAddValueRef(`0)
member.summary: Gets the value for the specified key, or, if the key is not present, adds an entry and returns the value by ref. This makes it possible to add or update a value in a single look up operation.
member.returns: Reference to the new or existing value

member.name: P:Jint.Engine.AdvancedOperations.StackTrace
member.summary: Gets current stack trace that is active in engine.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.AdvancedOperations.ResetCallStack
member.summary: Initializes list of references of called functions

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.AdvancedOperations.ProcessTasks
member.summary: Forcefully processes the current task queues (micro and regular), this API may break and change behavior!

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.AdvancedOperations.RegisterPromise
member.summary:
EXPERIMENTAL! Subject to change.
Registers a promise within the currently running EventLoop (has to be called within "ExecuteWithEventLoop" call). Note that ExecuteWithEventLoop will not trigger "onFinished" callback until ALL manual promises are settled.
NOTE: that resolve and reject need to be called withing the same thread as "ExecuteWithEventLoop". The API assumes that the Engine is called from a single thread.
member.returns: a Promise instance and functions to either resolve or reject it

member.name: E:Jint.Engine.AdvancedOperations.PromiseRejectionTracker
member.summary: Event raised when a promise is rejected without a handler (operation = Reject), or when a handler is added to a previously unhandled rejected promise (operation = Handle). This implements the HostPromiseRejectionTracker abstract operation from the TC39 spec.
member.remarks: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-hostpromiserejectiontracker

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.PrepareScript(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,Jint.ScriptPreparationOptions)
member.summary: Prepares a script for the engine that includes static analysis data to speed up execution during run-time.
member.remarks: Returned instance is reusable and thread-safe. You should prepare scripts only once and then reuse them.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.PrepareModule(System.String,System.String,Jint.ModulePreparationOptions)
member.summary: Prepares a module for the engine that includes static analysis data to speed up execution during run-time.
member.remarks: Returned instance is reusable and thread-safe. You should prepare modules only once and then reuse them.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.EvaluateAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Evaluates JavaScript code asynchronously, properly awaiting any promises. This is the non-blocking alternative to Evaluate() + UnwrapIfPromise(). During IO-bound operations (e.g., .NET Tasks awaited from JS), the calling thread is released and zero threads are consumed until work is available.
member.returns: The resolved value if the result is a promise, otherwise the direct result.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.EvaluateAsync(Jint.Prepared{Acornima.Ast.Script}@,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Evaluates a prepared script asynchronously, properly awaiting any promises.
member.returns: The resolved value if the result is a promise, otherwise the direct result.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.ExecuteAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Executes JavaScript code asynchronously, properly awaiting completion of any promises. This is the non-blocking alternative to Execute() when the code may contain async operations.
member.returns: The engine instance for chaining, after all async work completes.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.InvokeAsync(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Invokes a JavaScript function asynchronously, properly awaiting any returned promise.
member.param: The name of the function to invoke.
Arguments to pass to the function.
member.returns: The resolved value if the function returns a promise, otherwise the direct result.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.InvokeAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Object[])
member.summary: Invokes a JavaScript function asynchronously, properly awaiting any returned promise.
member.param: The name of the function to invoke.
Arguments to pass to the function.
member.returns: The resolved value if the function returns a promise, otherwise the direct result.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.UnwrapResultAsync(Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Core async unwrap: if the result is a JsPromise, awaits its settlement without blocking any thread. For non-promise values, returns synchronously.

member.name: M:Jint.Engine.AwaitPromiseSettlementAsync(Jint.Native.JsPromise,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Truly async promise settlement loop. Releases the thread between event loop processing cycles. When a .NET Task completes (e.g., gRPC IO), its ContinueWith callback enqueues work on the event loop and signals the wake, causing this method to resume on a thread pool thread, process the JS continuation, and either complete or go back to sleep if another await is hit.

member.name: M:Jint.Extensions.Hash.GetFNVHashCode(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Compute the hashcode of a sub-string using FNV-1a See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fowler%E2%80%93Noll%E2%80%93Vo_hash_function Note: FNV-1a was developed and tuned for 8-bit sequences. We're using it here for 16-bit Unicode chars on the understanding that the majority of chars will fit into 8-bits and, therefore, the algorithm will retain its desirable traits for generating hash codes.

member.returns: inner value if Promise the value itself otherwise

member.summary: If the value is a Promise 1. If "Fulfilled" returns the value it was fulfilled with 2. If "Rejected" throws "PromiseRejectedException" with the rejection reason 3. If "Pending" throws "OperationCanceledException" if cancellation is requested Else returns the value intact
member.returns: inner value if Promise the value itself otherwise

member.name: M:Jint.JsValueExtensions.UnwrapIfPromiseAsync(Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously unwraps a without blocking the calling thread. If the value is a Promise: 1. If "Fulfilled" returns the value it was fulfilled with 2. If "Rejected" throws with the rejection reason 3. If "Pending" awaits settlement asynchronously Else returns the value intact immediately.
member.returns: A task that resolves to the inner value if the value is a Promise, or the value itself otherwise

member.name: T:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionConstructor
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-async-function-constructor

member.name: T:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance
member.summary: Tracks the execution state of an async function, enabling suspension at await points and resumption when the awaited promise settles.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._savedContext
member.summary: The execution context captured when the async function started or last suspended. Used to restore the environment when resuming execution.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._capability
member.summary: The promise capability representing this async function's return value. Resolved when the function completes normally, rejected on exception.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._body
member.summary: The statement list being executed by this async function. Needed to resume execution from the saved position.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._bodyFunction
member.summary: The body function to execute (for expression bodies or statement list execution).

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._lastAwaitNode
member.summary: The AST node (AwaitExpression) where execution last suspended. Used for node-based tracking similar to generator yield tracking.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._resumeValue
member.summary: The value from the settled promise, to be returned when resuming at the await point.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._resumeWithThrow
member.summary: If true, the resumed await should throw the _resumeValue instead of returning it. Set when the awaited promise was rejected.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._isResuming
member.summary: Signals that we are resuming from a suspended await point. When true, the await expression at _lastAwaitNode should return _resumeValue.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance._completedAwaits
member.summary: Stores the resolved values of completed await expressions. For expression bodies that re-evaluate on resume, already-completed awaits return their cached values instead of re-suspending.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance.Jint#Runtime#ISuspendable#PendingCompletionType
member.summary: Tracks the pending completion type when suspended in a finally block.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance.Jint#Runtime#ISuspendable#PendingCompletionValue
member.summary: Tracks the pending completion value when suspended in a finally block.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionInstance.Jint#Runtime#ISuspendable#CurrentFinallyStatement
member.summary: The try statement whose finally block we're currently executing. Used to properly resume execution in finally blocks.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionState
member.summary: The execution state of an async function.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionState.Executing
member.summary: The async function is currently executing statements.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionState.SuspendedAwait
member.summary: The async function is suspended at an await expression, waiting for a promise to settle.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncFunction.AsyncFunctionState.Completed
member.summary: The async function has completed (either resolved or rejected its return promise).

member.name: T:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorRequest
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncgenerator-objects Represents a pending next/return/throw request for an async generator. Each request has a PromiseCapability that resolves when the request completes.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorRequest.#ctor(Jint.Runtime.Completion,Jint.Native.Promise.PromiseCapability)

member.name: T:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncgenerator-objects The execution state of an async generator.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState.SuspendedStart
member.summary: The async generator has been created but not yet started. Transitions to Executing on first next/return/throw call.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState.SuspendedYield
member.summary: The async generator is suspended at a yield expression. Transitions to Executing when resumed.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState.Executing
member.summary: The async generator is currently executing statements. Transitions to SuspendedYield (on yield), AwaitingReturn (on return), or Completed.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState.AwaitingReturn
member.summary: The async generator is awaiting a return value before completing. Transitions to Completed when the awaited promise settles.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.AsyncGenerator.AsyncGeneratorState.Completed
member.summary: The async generator has completed execution. All subsequent next() calls return {done: true}.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._isResuming
member.summary: Tracks whether we are resuming from a suspended yield (vs first call from SuspendedStart). When true, the first yield expression encountered should return _nextValue instead of yielding.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._suspendedValue
member.summary: Stores the value that was yielded when the generator suspended. This is needed because the statement containing the yield might have a different completion value (e.g., variable declarations return Empty, not the yielded value).

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._lastYieldNode
member.summary: The yield expression node we suspended at. Used for node-based yield tracking which works correctly for both loops (same node) and multi-yield expressions (different nodes).

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._yieldNodeValues
member.summary: Maps yield expression nodes to their return values from previous resumes. When re-executing code with yields (e.g., in loops), yields that have already been resumed return their stored value instead of yielding again.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._delegatingIterator
member.summary: The iterator we're delegating to during yield* evaluation. When this is non-null, we're in the middle of yield* delegation.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._delegatingYieldNode
member.summary: The yield* expression we're delegating from.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._delegationResumeType
member.summary: The type of completion we received when resuming during yield* delegation.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._delegationInnerResult
member.summary: When yield* suspends, stores the inner iterator's result object directly. This allows us to pass through the exact result (including its 'done' property state) instead of creating a new result with done: false.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._returnRequested
member.summary: Signals that generator.return() was called and execution should complete after running finally blocks. Aligns with spec's Return completion handling in GeneratorResumeAbrupt.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._resumeCompletionType
member.summary: The completion type used when resuming via GeneratorResumeAbrupt. Used by yield expressions to know if they should trigger an early return.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._pendingCompletionType
member.summary: Tracks a pending completion (throw/return) that needs to be processed after a finally block that yielded. When a finally block yields, we need to remember any pending completion so we can restore it after the finally block completes.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._pendingCompletionValue
member.summary: The value associated with the pending completion (the thrown error or return value).

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Generator.GeneratorInstance._currentFinallyStatement
member.summary: Tracks which try statement's finally block we're currently inside. Used to properly restore pending completion after finally completes.

member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-generatorstart

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper
member.summary: Helper class for Chinese and Dangi (Korean) calendar operations. Provides conversion from Gregorian dates to Chinese/Dangi calendar dates and computes the sexagenary cycle (干支 Gānzhī) year names.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.HeavenlyStems
member.summary: The 10 Heavenly Stems (天干 Tiāngān) in Chinese characters. Used as part of the 60-year sexagenary cycle.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.EarthlyBranches
member.summary: The 12 Earthly Branches (地支 Dìzhī) in Chinese characters. Used as part of the 60-year sexagenary cycle.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.GetChineseDate(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets Chinese calendar date information for a given DateTime.
member.returns: Chinese calendar date information including related year, year name, month, and day.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.GetDangiDate(System.DateTime)
member.summary: Gets Dangi (Korean lunisolar) calendar date information for a given DateTime. The Dangi calendar is essentially the same as the Chinese calendar.
member.returns: Dangi calendar date information including related year, year name, month, and day.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.GetSexagenaryYearName(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the sexagenary cycle year name (干支 Gānzhī) for a given sexagenary year number.
member.returns: The two-character Chinese year name.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate
member.summary: Represents a date in the Chinese or Dangi lunisolar calendar.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.#ctor(System.Int32,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new Chinese calendar date.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.RelatedYear
member.summary: The "related year" - the Gregorian year that mostly overlaps with this Chinese calendar year. For dates before Chinese New Year, this is the previous Gregorian year.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.YearName
member.summary: The Chinese sexagenary cycle name (干支 Gānzhī) for the year. Example: "己亥" (jǐ hài) for the year 2019.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.Month
member.summary: The month number in the Chinese calendar (1-12). Note: Leap months are indicated separately by IsLeapMonth.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.Day
member.summary: The day of the month in the Chinese calendar.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ChineseCalendarHelper.ChineseCalendarDate.IsLeapMonth
member.summary: Whether this date falls in a leap month. In lunisolar calendars, a leap month is an intercalary month inserted to keep the calendar aligned with the solar year.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.ListPatterns
member.summary: List formatting patterns from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ListPatterns.Start
member.summary: Pattern for the first pair in a list of 3+ items. E.g., "{0}, {1}".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ListPatterns.Middle
member.summary: Pattern for middle pairs in a list of 4+ items. E.g., "{0}, {1}".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ListPatterns.End
member.summary: Pattern for the last pair in a list of 3+ items. E.g., "{0}, and {1}".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.ListPatterns.Two
member.summary: Pattern for exactly two items. E.g., "{0} and {1}".

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns
member.summary: Relative time formatting patterns from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.Future
member.summary: Pattern for future singular. E.g., "in {0} day". Legacy property for backwards compatibility. Use FuturePatterns for full plural form support.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.Past
member.summary: Pattern for past singular. E.g., "{0} day ago". Legacy property for backwards compatibility. Use PastPatterns for full plural form support.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.FuturePlural
member.summary: Pattern for future plural. E.g., "in {0} days". Legacy property for backwards compatibility. Use FuturePatterns for full plural form support.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.PastPlural
member.summary: Pattern for past plural. E.g., "{0} days ago". Legacy property for backwards compatibility. Use PastPatterns for full plural form support.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.FuturePatterns
member.summary: Full plural form support for future patterns. Dictionary keyed by plural form (one, few, many, other). If set, this takes precedence over Future/FuturePlural.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.RelativeTimePatterns.PastPatterns
member.summary: Full plural form support for past patterns. Dictionary keyed by plural form (one, few, many, other). If set, this takes precedence over Past/PastPlural.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.CurrencyData
member.summary: Currency formatting data from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.CurrencyData.Symbol
member.summary: Currency symbol. E.g., "$".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.CurrencyData.NarrowSymbol
member.summary: Narrow currency symbol. E.g., "$" instead of "US$".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.CurrencyData.DisplayName
member.summary: Display name for the currency. E.g., "US Dollar".

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns
member.summary: Unit formatting patterns from CLDR. Supports all CLDR plural categories: zero, one, two, few, many, other.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.DisplayName
member.summary: Display name for the unit. E.g., "meters".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.Zero
member.summary: Pattern for zero (some languages). E.g., "{0} ימים" in Hebrew for 0 days.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.One
member.summary: Pattern for singular. E.g., "{0} meter".

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.Two
member.summary: Pattern for dual (some languages like Arabic). E.g., "{0} يومان" for 2 days.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.Few
member.summary: Pattern for few (some languages like Polish, Russian). E.g., "{0} dni" for 3-4 days.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.Many
member.summary: Pattern for many (some languages like Polish, Russian). E.g., "{0} dni" for 5+ days.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.UnitPatterns.Other
member.summary: Pattern for plural/other. E.g., "{0} meters". Required fallback.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.CompactPatterns
member.summary: Compact number formatting patterns from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.CompactPatterns.Patterns
member.summary: Patterns keyed by magnitude (3 for thousands, 6 for millions, etc.). Value is the pattern, e.g., "{0}K" or "{0} thousand".

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimePatterns
member.summary: Date/time formatting patterns from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimePatterns.DatePattern
member.summary: Date pattern component.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimePatterns.TimePattern
member.summary: Time pattern component.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimePatterns.DateTimePattern
member.summary: Combined date/time pattern.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.WeekInfo
member.summary: Week information from CLDR.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.WeekInfo.FirstDay
member.summary: First day of the week.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.WeekInfo.MinimalDays
member.summary: Minimal days in first week of year.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.WeekInfo.Weekend
member.summary: Weekend days.

member.summary: Implements CLDR Add Likely Subtags and Remove Likely Subtags algorithms. https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/#Likely_Subtags

member.summary: Add Likely Subtags algorithm - maximizes a locale by adding likely script and region.

member.summary: Provides CLDR list formatting patterns for locale-aware list formatting. Data is loaded from embedded ListPatternsData.txt resource.

member.summary: Gets list patterns for a locale. Returns patterns grouped by type_style (e.g., "conjunction_long", "unit_narrow"). Falls back to English if locale not found.

member.summary: Provides CLDR locale data for canonicalization. Data is loaded from embedded LocaleData.txt resource.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.TagMappings
member.summary: Grandfathered tag mappings (e.g., "art-lojban" → "jbo").

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.LanguageMappings
member.summary: Simple language code mappings (e.g., "cmn" → "zh", "iw" → "he").

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.ComplexLanguageMappings
member.summary: Complex language mappings that may include script/region.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.RegionMappings
member.summary: Region code mappings (e.g., "DD" → "DE", numeric to alpha).

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.VariantMappings
member.summary: Variant subtag mappings.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.LanguageVariantMappings
member.summary: Language + variant mappings for grandfathered variants. Key format: "language+variant" (e.g., "art+lojban"), Value: new language (e.g., "jbo").

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.ScriptRegionMappings
member.summary: Script + region mappings for deprecated regions (like SU). Key format: "Script+Region" (e.g., "Armn+SU"), Value: new region (e.g., "AM").

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.UnicodeMappings
member.summary: Unicode extension value mappings, keyed by extension key (e.g., "ca", "tz").

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.LocaleData.VariantMapping.Prefix
member.summary: Optional prefix variant that should be removed when this alias is applied.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.MetaZoneData.IanaToMetaZone
member.summary: Maps IANA timezone IDs to their current CLDR metazone name.

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.MetaZoneData.EnUsNames
member.summary: En-US display names for metazones. From CLDR dates/timeZoneNames for en-US locale.

member.param: Whether daylight saving time is currently in effect
True for long format, false for short format
member.returns: The display name, or null if not found in CLDR data

member.summary: Canonical IANA timezone identifiers (Zone names only - primary identifiers). These are returned by Intl.supportedValuesOf("timeZone"). Does NOT include Link names or UTC aliases (Etc/GMT, Etc/UTC, GMT).

member.name: F:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.TimeZoneData.AllTimeZones
member.summary: All valid IANA timezone identifiers (Zone names AND Link names). This list is used for validation and case-insensitive lookup. Includes Link names that are valid input but may not be returned by supportedValuesOf.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.TimeZoneData.GetAllTimeZones
member.summary: Gets all supported IANA timezone identifiers (Zone names and Link names). Used for validation and case-insensitive lookup.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.TimeZoneData.GetCanonicalTimeZones
member.summary: Gets only canonical (primary) timezone identifiers. Used by Intl.supportedValuesOf("timeZone"). Does not include Link names or UTC aliases (Etc/GMT, Etc/UTC, GMT).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.TimeZoneData.FindCanonical(System.String)
member.summary: Performs a case-insensitive lookup of a timezone identifier. Returns the canonical (properly-cased) form if found, null otherwise.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.Data.TimeZoneData.IsSupported(System.String)

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.TryParseOffsetTimeZone(System.String)
member.summary: Parses and canonicalizes an offset time zone string. Valid formats: "+HH", "-HH", "+HH:MM", "-HH:MM", "+HHMM", "-HHMM" Returns canonical form like "+03:00" or "-07:30", or null if not a valid offset.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.SupportedLocalesOf(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.supportedlocalesof

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.GetDefaultCalendarForLocale(System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Gets the default calendar for a locale. Per ECMA-402, most locales use "gregory". Japanese locale uses "japanese", Thai uses "buddhist", etc.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.IsValidCalendar(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the given calendar is supported.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.BuildResolvedLocale(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Builds the resolved locale string, including only the unicode extensions that should be preserved.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.CanonicalizeCalendar(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes calendar names per ECMA-402. Converts deprecated/alias names to their canonical forms. Per ECMA-402, "islamic" and "islamic-rgsa" should fallback to a valid calendar from AvailableCalendars (like "islamic-civil").

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatConstructor.ExtractUnicodeExtensionFromLocale(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Extracts a specific unicode extension value from a single locale string.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-properties-of-intl-datetimeformat-prototype-object

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.GetFormat(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.prototype.format

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.FormatToParts(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.prototype.formattoparts

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ToDateTimeFormattable(Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-todatetimeformattable Returns the Temporal object as-is if it is a Temporal object, otherwise converts to Number.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.IsTemporalObject(Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Returns true if the value is any Temporal type.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ValidateTemporalRangeTypes(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Validates that range arguments are of the same Temporal type. Per spec: If IsTemporalObject(x) or IsTemporalObject(y), then SameTemporalType(x, y) must be true.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ValidateTemporalCalendarsMatch(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Validates that two Temporal objects with calendars use the same calendar. Per spec: formatRange throws RangeError if calendars differ.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.HandleDateTimeNonTemporal(Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat,Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Handles a non-temporal formattable value (Date, Number, undefined).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.GetTemporalFormatDtf(Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat,Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Per spec HandleDateTimeValue + GetDateTimeFormat: computes the per-type format DTF for a Temporal object. Each Temporal type uses a specific format record with its own required/defaults, which may differ from the main DateTimeFormat record.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.CreatePerTypeFormatDtfFiltered(Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat,Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeRequired,Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeDefaults,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a per-type format DTF by filtering components relevant to the temporal type. Implements GetDateTimeFormat with inherit=~relevant~. When HasExplicitFormatComponents is true: filter to relevant components, throw if no overlap. When HasExplicitFormatComponents is false: apply per-type defaults (e.g., era-only case).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ConvertTemporalToDateTime(Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Converts a Temporal object to a .NET DateTime for formatting. When the year is outside .NET's 1-9999 range, a representative year is used that preserves leap year status and day-of-week, and the original year is returned.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.CreateDateTimeSafe(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Creates a DateTime, using a representative year if the actual year is outside .NET's 1-9999 range. The representative year preserves leap year status and day-of-week alignment (Gregorian 400-year cycle).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.IsLeapYearISO(System.Int32)
member.summary: ISO 8601 leap year calculation (works for negative years).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.HandleDateTimeTemporalOrOtherForRange(Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat,Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Handles a formattable value for formatRange/formatRangeToParts.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ValidateTemporalStyleRestrictions(Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Validates style restrictions for Temporal "date-only" and "time-only" types. PlainDate/PlainYearMonth/PlainMonthDay: timeStyle without dateStyle → TypeError PlainTime: dateStyle without timeStyle → TypeError When both styles present: the "wrong" one is simply ignored by the formatting.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.IsoDateToDateTime(Jint.Native.Temporal.IsoDate,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Converts an IsoDate to DateTime (time set to noon for formatting). Uses representative year for out-of-range years.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.IsoDateTimeToDateTime(Jint.Native.Temporal.IsoDateTime,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Converts an IsoDateTime to DateTime. Uses representative year for out-of-range years.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.IsoTimeToDateTime(Jint.Native.Temporal.IsoTime)
member.summary: Converts an IsoTime to DateTime using reference date 1970-01-01.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.EpochNanosecondsToDateTime(System.Numerics.BigInteger)
member.summary: Converts epoch nanoseconds (BigInteger) to DateTime in UTC.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ToDateTimeWithOriginalYear(Jint.Native.JsValue,System.Nullable{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Converts a JavaScript value to DateTime, returning the original JavaScript year when the date is outside .NET DateTime range (for proper era formatting).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.GetJavaScriptYear(System.Double)
member.summary: Extracts the JavaScript year from a timestamp value using the ECMAScript algorithm. JavaScript Date uses milliseconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.DayFromYear(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the day number of the first day of a given year (ECMAScript algorithm).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.GetDefaultHourCycle(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the default hour cycle for a locale. Most locales use h12, but some use h23 (24-hour format without leading zero for midnight).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ResolvedOptions(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.prototype.resolvedoptions

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.FormatRange(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.prototype.formatrange

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.FormatRangeToParts(Jint.Native.JsValue,Jint.Native.JsValue[])
member.summary: https://tc39.es/ecma402/#sec-intl.datetimeformat.prototype.formatrangetoparts

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.FindNaturalBoundaryPrefix(System.Collections.Generic.List{Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat.DateTimePart},System.Collections.Generic.List{Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat.DateTimePart})
member.summary: Finds the length of the shared prefix between start and end parts, but only if the prefix ends at a natural boundary (e.g., the ", " literal separating date from time components, or a multi-char literal separator). Internal separators like "/" and ":" within date/time groups are not natural boundaries. Also ensures the remaining unique part has at least 2 non-literal components to avoid over-collapsing (e.g., collapsing "Mar 4, " when only year differs). Returns 0 if no valid shared prefix exists.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.FindNaturalBoundarySuffix(System.Collections.Generic.List{Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat.DateTimePart},System.Collections.Generic.List{Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat.DateTimePart},System.Int32)
member.summary: Finds the number of shared suffix parts between start and end parts, starting from the given offset (to avoid overlapping with a shared prefix). Only accepts suffixes that start at a natural boundary (multi-char literal). Returns 0 if no valid shared suffix exists.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.DateTimeFormatPrototype.ToIanaTimeZoneId(System.String)
member.summary: Converts a timezone ID to IANA format. Windows uses names like "FLE Standard Time" but ECMA-402 requires IANA names like "Europe/Helsinki".

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.DefaultCldrProvider
member.summary: Default CLDR provider that uses embedded data files and hardcoded patterns. Supports basic en-US/en-GB with English fallback for other locales.

member.summary: Singleton instance of the default provider.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Intl.ICldrProvider
member.summary: Provider interface for CLDR (Common Locale Data Repository) data. Implementations can supply locale-specific data for Intl formatters.

member.returns: List patterns or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets relative time formatting patterns for a locale.
member.returns: Relative time patterns or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets a special relative time phrase (e.g., "yesterday", "tomorrow", "last week").
member.param: Whether the value represents past time.
member.returns: Special phrase or null if not available (fallback to numeric format).

member.summary: Gets the digit characters for a numbering system.
member.returns: A string of 10 digit characters (0-9) or null if not supported.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ICldrProvider.GetCompactPatterns(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Gets compact number patterns for a locale.
member.returns: Compact patterns or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets currency data for a locale and currency code.
member.returns: Currency data or null if not available.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ICldrProvider.GetUnitPatterns(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Gets unit formatting patterns for a locale.
member.returns: Unit patterns or null if not available.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.ICldrProvider.GetDateTimePatterns(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Gets date/time formatting patterns for a locale.
member.returns: DateTime patterns or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets month names for a locale.
member.param: Calendar identifier (e.g., "gregory", "buddhist"), or null for default.
member.returns: Array of 12 month names (January-December) or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets weekday names for a locale.
member.returns: Array of 7 weekday names (Sunday-Saturday) or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets day period names (AM/PM) for a locale.
member.param: Calendar identifier (e.g., "gregory", "buddhist"), or null for default.
member.returns: Array of day period names or null if not available.

member.summary: Gets era names for a locale.
member.param: Calendar identifier (e.g., "gregory", "japanese"), or null for default.
member.returns: Array of era names or null if not available.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.JsDateTimeFormat.ComputeHourValue(System.Int32,System.String@,System.Boolean@,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the formatted hour string based on the hourCycle, hour option, and actual hour value. Returns the formatted hour string and whether AM/PM should be shown. Per ECMA-402: h11=0-11 (12hr), h12=1-12 (12hr), h23=0-23 (24hr), h24=1-24 (24hr). 24-hour formats always pad to 2 digits; 12-hour formats pad only for "2-digit" option.
Computes the formatted hour value based on HourCycle and locale defaults.
member.param: Output: whether 12-hour format is used (needs AM/PM)
If true, always pad h23/h24 hours (used by style-based formatting)

member.summary: Returns the formatted parts with their types for formatToParts.
member.param: Optional original JavaScript year (for dates outside .NET DateTime range)
If true, skip timezone conversion (for plain Temporal types)

CLDR list patterns use different templates based on position: - start: "{0}, {1}" (first two items) - middle: "{0}, {1}" (middle items) - end: "{0}, and {1}" (last two items) - two: "{0} and {1}" (exactly two items - no comma before "and")

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.GetVariantsOption(Jint.Native.Object.ObjectInstance,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String})
member.summary: Gets and validates the variants option. The option value is a string of hyphen-separated variant subtags. Variants are sorted alphabetically for canonicalization.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.GetFirstDayOfWeekOption(Jint.Native.Object.ObjectInstance,System.String)
member.summary: Gets and validates the firstDayOfWeek option. Numeric values (0-7) are converted to weekday strings. String values must be 3-8 alphanumeric characters (can be hyphenated).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.WeekdayToString(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a weekday number to its string representation. 0 and 7 = sun, 1 = mon, 2 = tue, 3 = wed, 4 = thu, 5 = fri, 6 = sat

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.IsValidFirstDayOfWeekValue(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a string matches the firstDayOfWeek value pattern. Pattern: (3*8alphanum) *("-" (3*8alphanum)) Each part must be 3-8 alphanumeric characters.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.IsVariantSubtag(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string is a valid variant subtag. Variant subtags are 5-8 alphanumerics OR 4 chars starting with a digit.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.GetUnicodeExtensionKey(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the key (first 2-char subtag) from a Unicode extension part like "ca-gregory" or "kn".

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.CollectMultiPartValue(System.String[],System.Int32@,System.String)
member.summary: Collects multi-part Unicode extension values (e.g., "islamic-civil" for calendar). Values are 3-8 alphanumeric characters, and 2-char parts indicate a new key.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.ParsedLocale.Attributes
member.summary: Unicode extension attributes (3-8 alphanumeric subtags before any keys).

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.ParsedLocale.OtherUnicodeExtensions
member.summary: Stores unrecognized unicode extension keys/values (e.g., "cu-eur", etc.)

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.ParsedLocale.OtherExtensions
member.summary: Stores other extensions as (singleton, content) pairs for sorting. e.g., ('a', "bar"), ('x', "u-foo")

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.CanonicalizeGrandfatheredTag(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes grandfathered tags using CLDR data.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.CanonicalizeLanguage(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes a language subtag using CLDR data.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.ApplyVariantMappings(System.String@,System.String@,System.String@,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String}@)
member.summary: Applies variant aliasing per CLDR variantAlias rules. For example: - arevela → changes language to "hy" (and removes the variant) - aaland → changes region to "AX" (and removes the variant) - heploc → changes to variant "alalc97"

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Intl.LocaleConstructor.ApplyLanguageVariantMappings(System.String@,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String}@)
member.summary: Applies language+variant mappings for grandfathered variants. For example, "art" + "lojban" → "jbo" (with "lojban" removed from variants).

member.summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/es-shims/Math.sumPrecise/main/sum.js adapted from https://github.com/tc39/proposal-math-sum/blob/f4286d0a9d8525bda61be486df964bf2527c8789/polyfill/polyfill.mjs https://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs/project/quake/public/papers/robust-arithmetic.ps Shewchuk's algorithm for exactly floating point addition as implemented in Python's fsum: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/48dfd74a9db9d4aa9c6f23b4a67b461e5d977173/Modules/mathmodule.c#L1359-L1474 adapted to handle overflow via an additional "biased" partial, representing 2**1024 times its actual value

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Number.NumberIntlHelper.GetDecimalDigitCount(System.Double)
member.summary: Checks the powers of 10 of number to count the number of decimal digits. Returns a clamped JS_MAX_DECIMAL_DIGIT_COUNT count. JavaScript will use the shortest representation that accurately represents the value and clamp the decimal digits to JS_MAX_DECIMAL_DIGIT_COUNT. C# fills the digits with zeros up to the culture's numberFormat.NumberDecimalDigits and does not provide the same max (numberFormat.NumberDecimalDigits != JS_MAX_DECIMAL_DIGIT_COUNT). This function matches the JS behaviour for the decimal digits returned, this is the actual decimal digits for a number (with no zeros fill) clamped to JS_MAX_DECIMAL_DIGIT_COUNT.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.ToLowerCaseWithSpecialCasing(System.String,System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Converts string to lowercase with Unicode special casing rules. Handles Final_Sigma, Turkish/Azeri I-dot, Lithuanian soft-dotted, and İ decomposition. https://unicode.org/reports/tr21/tr21-5.html#SpecialCasing

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.NeedsSpecialCasing(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the string needs special casing beyond standard ToLower.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.FollowedByDotAbove(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if character at index i is followed by U+0307 (COMBINING DOT ABOVE) with only characters of combining class less than 230 in between. Used for Turkish/Azeri I + dot above handling.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.FollowedByCombiningClass230(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if character at index i is followed by a combining mark with combining class 230. Used for Lithuanian soft-dotted character handling.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.GetCombiningClass(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the Unicode combining class for a code point. Covers the Combining Diacritical Marks block and common supplementary combining marks.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.IsFinalSigmaContext(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Determines if the character at the given position is in a Final_Sigma context. Final_Sigma: C is preceded by a sequence consisting of a cased letter and then zero or more Case_Ignorable characters, and C is NOT followed by a sequence consisting of zero or more Case_Ignorable characters and then a cased letter. https://unicode.org/reports/tr21/tr21-5.html#Context

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.IsCased(System.Char)
member.summary: Checks if a character is "cased" (has uppercase or lowercase property). A character is cased if it has the Lowercase or Uppercase property, or has General_Category=Titlecase_Letter.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.String.StringPrototype.IsCaseIgnorable(System.Char)
member.summary: Checks if a character is Case_Ignorable. Case_Ignorable characters include: Mn (Nonspacing_Mark), Me (Enclosing_Mark), Cf (Format), Lm (Modifier_Letter), Sk (Modifier_Symbol), and characters with Word_Break property MidLetter, MidNumLet, or Single_Quote.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Temporal.DefaultTimeZoneProvider
member.summary: Default time zone provider using .NET TimeZoneInfo.
member.remarks: This provides basic IANA time zone support via .NET's built-in TimeZoneInfo class. On Windows, Windows time zone IDs are mapped to IANA identifiers where possible. For full IANA support, consider using TimeZoneConverter package with a custom provider.

member.name: P:Jint.Native.Temporal.DefaultTimeZoneProvider.Instance
member.summary: Singleton instance of the default provider.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.DefaultTimeZoneProvider.GetPrimaryTimeZoneIdentifier(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the primary IANA identifier for a timezone, resolving aliases. E.g., "Asia/Calcutta" → "Asia/Kolkata", "America/Atka" → "America/Adak".

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.DefaultTimeZoneProvider.ParseOffsetString(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an offset string like "+01:00" or "-05:30" to a TimeSpan.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.DefaultTimeZoneProvider.IsValidIanaIdentifier(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a timezone identifier follows IANA naming conventions. Rejects Java/ICU-specific abbreviations like ACT, BST, JST that .NET may resolve.

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider
member.summary: Interface for pluggable time zone support in Temporal API. This allows users to provide custom time zone implementations (e.g., using TimeZoneConverter or NodaTime).
member.remarks: https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-timezone-objects

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetOffsetNanosecondsFor(System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger)
member.summary: Gets the UTC offset in nanoseconds for a specific instant in a time zone.
member.param: The instant as nanoseconds since Unix epoch.
member.returns: The UTC offset in nanoseconds.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetPossibleInstantsFor(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets possible instant(s) for an ambiguous local date/time in a time zone. Returns multiple values during DST "fall back" (ambiguous time), or empty during DST "spring forward" (skipped time).
member.returns: Array of possible epoch nanosecond values.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetNextTransition(System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger)
member.summary: Gets the epoch nanoseconds of the next DST transition after the given instant.
member.returns: The epoch nanoseconds of the next transition, or null if none.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetPreviousTransition(System.String,System.Numerics.BigInteger)
member.summary: Gets the epoch nanoseconds of the previous DST transition before the given instant.
member.returns: The epoch nanoseconds of the previous transition, or null if none.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.IsValidTimeZone(System.String)
member.summary: Validates whether a time zone identifier is recognized.
member.param: The time zone identifier to validate.
member.returns: True if the identifier is valid.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.CanonicalizeTimeZone(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes a time zone identifier to its IANA canonical form.
member.param: The time zone identifier to canonicalize.
member.returns: The canonical IANA identifier, or null if invalid.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetAvailableTimeZones
member.summary: Gets all available time zone identifiers.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetDefaultTimeZone
member.summary: Gets the system's default time zone identifier.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.ITimeZoneProvider.GetPrimaryTimeZoneIdentifier(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a time zone alias to its primary IANA identifier. E.g., "Asia/Calcutta" → "Asia/Kolkata", "US/Eastern" → "America/New_York". Returns null if the time zone ID is not recognized.

member.summary: Parses a pure time string (with optional annotations) according to the Temporal spec.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.PlainTimeConstructor.ParseTimeFromDateTimeString(System.String)
member.summary: Parses a PlainTime string from a date-time string according to the Temporal spec. Returns null for invalid strings, but may also return error for specific violations.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.PlainTimeConstructor.HasUtcDesignator(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the string contains a UTC designator (Z) that is not inside a bracket annotation.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.PlainTimeConstructor.IsAmbiguousTimeString(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a time string (without T prefix) is ambiguous with date formats. Ambiguous strings could be valid dates and require a "T" prefix for PlainTime. Based on TC39 Temporal spec requirement for disambiguation.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.PlainTimeConstructor.IsValidMonthDay(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if a month-day combination is valid (exists in at least some years). Used to determine if a string like "0230" is ambiguous (could be a date) or unambiguous (can only be time). Per spec: "0229" is ambiguous (Feb 29 exists in leap years), but "0230" is unambiguous (Feb 30 never exists).

member.name: T:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers
member.summary: Shared helper functions for Temporal types.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.PadIsoYear(System.Int32)
member.summary: Formats an ISO year as 4-digit (0000-9999) or 6-digit with sign prefix.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ContainsInvalidMinusSign(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a string doesn't contain non-ASCII minus sign (U+2212). Only ASCII hyphen-minus (U+002D) is valid in ISO 8601 strings.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseOffsetString(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an offset string like "+01:00" or "-05:30" to nanoseconds.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.FormatOffsetRounded(System.Int64)
member.summary: FormatDateTimeUTCOffsetRounded: Rounds offset to nearest minute and formats as ±HH:MM. Used by ZonedDateTime.toString() and Instant.toString() per the Temporal spec.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.FormatOffsetString(System.Int64)
member.summary: Formats an offset in nanoseconds as an ISO 8601 offset string (full precision).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseDuration(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an ISO 8601 duration string.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.NoNegativeZero(System.Double)
member.summary: Ensures a double value of 0 is positive zero, not negative zero. Mathematical values (ℝ) in the spec don't have a concept of -0.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.StripAnnotations(System.String,System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Strips annotations (calendar, timezone, etc.) from an ISO string and returns the core string and calendar. Handles: [u-ca=calendar], [timezone], [!u-ca=calendar], etc. Returns error message if annotations are invalid, null if valid.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsLowercaseAnnotationKey(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if an annotation key is valid (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseIsoDate(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an ISO 8601 date string.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseIsoTime(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an ISO 8601 time string.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidTimeWithOffset(System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a string is a well-formed time component with optional offset. Used to validate the time portion of date-time strings for PlainDate.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseInstantString(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an ISO 8601 instant string and returns epoch nanoseconds. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-parsetemporalinstantstring

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidDuration(Jint.Native.Temporal.DurationRecord)
member.summary: Validates that a duration has valid components. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-isvalidduration

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.TotalDurationNanoseconds(Jint.Native.Temporal.DurationRecord)
member.summary: Converts a duration to total nanoseconds (excluding calendar units). Only valid for durations without years, months, or weeks.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ToSingularUnit(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the singular form of a temporal unit name.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidDateTimeUnit(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string is a valid temporal unit for date-time rounding.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsTimeUnit(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a unit is a time unit (hour, minute, second, millisecond, microsecond, nanosecond). Per spec: TemporalUnitCategory(unit) is ~time~

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidTemporalUnit(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string is a valid temporal unit (including date units).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidTimeUnit(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string is a valid temporal unit for time rounding.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.HasNegativeZeroYear(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string starts with a negative zero year (-000000).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsOffsetString(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a string looks like an offset (starts with +, -, or Unicode minus).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsSubMinuteOffset(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if an offset string contains sub-minute (seconds) components. Valid offsets are ±HH, ±HHMM, or ±HH:MM only (max 6 chars including sign).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ExtractOffsetFromIsoDateTime(System.String)
member.summary: Extracts the UTC offset from an ISO date-time string (the portion before any bracket). Scans backward for +/- that indicates the start of an offset.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ValidateTimeZoneId(Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Realm,System.String)
member.summary: Validates a time zone ID against the provider and returns the case-corrected form. Per spec, returns [[Identifier]] (not [[PrimaryIdentifier]]). Throws RangeError if the ID is not a valid time zone. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-totemporaltimezoneidentifier

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.TimeZoneEquals(Jint.Engine,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Compares two time zone identifiers for equality per TC39 TimeZoneEquals. Resolves IANA aliases to their primary identifiers before comparing. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-timezoneequals

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ParseTemporalTimeZoneString(Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Realm,System.String)
member.summary: Parses a time zone string and returns the canonical time zone identifier. Handles IANA IDs, offset strings, and ISO date-time strings with embedded time zones. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-parsetemporaltimezonestring

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ToTemporalTimeZoneIdentifier(Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Realm,Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Converts a JS value to a temporal time zone identifier string. Requires the value to be a string; throws TypeError for non-strings. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-totemporaltimezoneidentifier

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidRoundingMode(System.String)
member.summary: Valid rounding modes per the Temporal spec.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidIsoDateTime(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates that a datetime is within valid ISO limits. https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-isvalidisodate

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsValidIsoDateTime(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates that a date-time is within Temporal's supported range. For PlainDateTime, the range is more restrictive than PlainDate: Minimum: -271821-04-19T00:00:00.000000001 (NOT midnight!) Maximum: +275760-09-13T23:59:59.999999999

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ISOYearMonthWithinLimits(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates that a year-month is within Temporal's supported range. Range: -271821-04 to +275760-09 https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-isoyearmonthwithinlimits

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CanonicalizeCalendar(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes a calendar identifier (case-insensitive matching). https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/#sec-temporal-canonicalizetemporalcalendaridentifier

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CalendarDateToISO(Jint.Runtime.Realm,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Converts calendar-system year/month/day to ISO year/month/day. For non-ISO calendars, the input fields are in the calendar's system and need to be converted to the proleptic Gregorian (ISO 8601) calendar.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.FindCalendarReferenceYear(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: For PlainMonthDay without an explicit year: finds a calendar year such that converting (calendarYear, month, day) to ISO produces an ISO date with the given ISO reference year (typically 1972).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IslamicCivilDaysInMonth(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the number of days in a given month of the Islamic Civil (tabular) calendar. Odd months have 30 days, even months have 29 days. Month 12 has 30 days in leap years.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsIslamicCivilLeapYear(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns whether the given year is a leap year in the Islamic Civil calendar. Uses the 30-year cycle: years 2, 5, 7, 10, 13, 16, 18, 21, 24, 26, 29 are leap years.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IslamicCivilDateToISO(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Converts an Islamic Civil (tabular) calendar date to ISO date via Julian Day Number. The Islamic Civil calendar uses a fixed 30-year cycle with known leap year positions. Epoch: July 16, 622 CE Julian (civil epoch, Friday).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IslamicTblaDateToISO(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Converts an Islamic Tabular (Thursday epoch) calendar date to ISO date via Julian Day Number. Similar to Islamic Civil but with a different epoch (Thursday instead of Friday).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IslamicToJulianDay(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int64)
member.summary: Computes the Julian Day Number for an Islamic calendar date with the given epoch.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.JulianDayToISO(System.Int64)
member.summary: Converts a Julian Day Number to an ISO (proleptic Gregorian) date. Uses the standard algorithm for JDN to Gregorian conversion.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.IsGregorianBasedCalendar(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the calendar uses the same Gregorian (proleptic) arithmetic as iso8601. These calendars differ only in era/epoch, not in month/day/year calculations.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CalendarUsesEras(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the calendar supports era/eraYear fields.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CalendarEra(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the era string for a given calendar and ISO year, or null if not supported.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CalendarEraYear(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the eraYear for a given calendar and ISO year, or null if not supported.

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.CalendarYear(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the calendar-specific year for Gregorian-based calendars. For non-Gregorian-based calendars, returns the ISO year unchanged (best effort).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ReadEraFields(Jint.Runtime.Realm,Jint.Native.Object.ObjectInstance,System.String)
member.summary: Reads era and eraYear properties from a property bag for era-supporting calendars. Returns the computed year if era/eraYear are present, or null if they should be ignored. Throws TypeError if only one of era/eraYear is present. Throws RangeError if eraYear is invalid (Infinity, NaN, etc.).

member.name: M:Jint.Native.Temporal.TemporalHelpers.ComputeYearFromEra(Jint.Runtime.Realm,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the ISO year from a calendar-specific era and eraYear. Throws RangeError if the era is not valid for the given calendar.

member.summary: Checks if a string looks like an ISO date/datetime/time string.

member.summary: Extracts and canonicalizes the calendar from a string, defaulting to "iso8601".

member.summary: Converts BigInteger to double (𝔽 operation in the spec). Implements IEEE 754 round-to-nearest-even, which .NET's (double)BigInteger does NOT guarantee (it may round toward zero for values larger than long.MaxValue).

member.name: P:Jint.Options.Strict
member.summary: Whether the code should be always considered to be in strict mode. Can improve performance.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.Culture
member.summary: The culture the engine runs on, defaults to current culture.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.TimeSystem
member.summary: Configures a time system to use. Defaults to DefaultTimeSystem using local time.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.TimeZone
member.summary: The time zone the engine runs on, defaults to local. Same as setting DefaultTimeSystem with the time zone.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ReferenceResolver
member.summary: Reference resolver allows customizing behavior for reference resolving. This can be useful in cases where you want to ignore long chain of property accesses that might throw if anything is null or undefined. An example of such is var a = obj.field.subField.value. Custom resolver could accept chain to return null/undefined on first occurrence.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.StringCompilationAllowed
member.summary: Whether calling 'eval' with custom code and function constructors taking function code as string is allowed. Defaults to true.
member.remarks: https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-hostensurecancompilestrings

member.name: P:Jint.Options.Json
member.summary: Options for the built-in JSON (de)serializer which gets used using JSON.parse or JSON.stringify

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ExperimentalFeatures
member.summary: What experimental features are allowed, functionality may lacking or even plain wrong. Defaults to having none.

member.name: M:Jint.Options.Apply(Jint.Engine)
member.summary: Called by the instance that loads this once it is loaded.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.DebuggerOptions.Enabled
member.summary: Whether debugger functionality is enabled, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.DebuggerOptions.StatementHandling
member.summary: Configures the statement handling strategy, defaults to Ignore.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.DebuggerOptions.InitialStepMode
member.summary: Configures the step mode used when entering the script.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.Enabled
member.summary: Whether accessing CLR and it's types and methods is allowed from JS code, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AllowGetType
member.summary: Whether to expose which can allow bypassing allow lists and open a way to reflection. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AllowSystemReflection
member.summary: Whether Jint should allow wrapping objects from System.Reflection namespace. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AllowWrite
member.summary: Whether writing to CLR objects is allowed (set properties), defaults to true.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AllowOperatorOverloading
member.summary: Whether operator overloading resolution is allowed, defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ExtensionMethodTypes
member.summary: Types holding extension methods that should be considered when resolving methods.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ObjectConverters
member.summary: Object converters to try when build-in conversions.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.TrackObjectWrapperIdentity
member.summary: Whether identity map is persisted for object wrappers in order to maintain object identity. This can cause memory usage to grow when targeting large set and freeing of memory can be delayed due to ConditionalWeakTable semantics. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.WrapObjectHandler
member.summary: If no known type could be guessed, objects are by default wrapped as an ObjectInstance using class ObjectWrapper. This function can be used to change the behavior.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.BuildCallStackHandler
member.summary: The handler used to build stack traces. Changing this enables mapping stack traces to code different from the code being executed, eg. when executing code transpiled from TypeScript.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ExceptionHandler
member.summary: Exceptions that thrown from CLR code are converted to JavaScript errors and can be used in at try/catch statement. By default these exceptions are bubbled to the CLR host and interrupt the script execution. If handler returns true these exceptions are converted to JS errors that can be caught by the script.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ClrExceptionErrorDecorator
member.summary: Called after a JavaScript error object is created from a CLR exception (when returns true). Allows decorating the error object with additional properties or modifying its state. The decorator receives the engine instance, the created error object, and the original CLR exception.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AllowedAssemblies
member.summary: Assemblies to allow scripts to call CLR types directly like System.IO.File.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.TypeResolver
member.summary: Type and member resolving strategy, which allows filtering allowed members and configuring member name matching comparison.
member.remarks: As this object holds caching state same instance should be shared between engines, if possible.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ValueCoercion
member.summary: When writing values to CLR objects, how should JS values be coerced to CLR types. Defaults to only coercing to string values when writing to string targets.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.CreateClrObject
member.summary: Strategy to create a CLR object to hold converted .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.CreateTypeReferenceObject
member.summary: Strategy to create a CLR object from TypeReference. Defaults to retuning null which makes TypeReference attempt to find suitable constructor.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.SerializeToJson
member.summary: When not null, is used to serialize any CLR object in an passing through 'JSON.stringify'.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.DateTimeKind
member.summary: What kind of date time should be produced when JavaScript date is converted to DateTime. If Local, uses . Defaults to .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.AttachArrayPrototype
member.summary: Should the Array prototype be attached instead of Object prototype to the wrapped interop objects when type looks suitable. Defaults to true.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ThrowOnUnresolvedMember
member.summary: Whether the engine should throw an error when a member is not found on a CLR object. Defaults to false.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ObjectWrapperReportedMemberTypes
member.summary: Types of CLR members reported by when enumerating properties/serializing . Supported values are: , , . All other values are ignored.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ObjectWrapperReportedFieldBindingFlags
member.summary: Reported member binding flags when reflecting, defaults to | .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ObjectWrapperReportedPropertyBindingFlags
member.summary: Reported member binding flags when reflecting, defaults to | .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.InteropOptions.ObjectWrapperReportedMethodBindingFlags

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.Constraints
member.summary: Registered constraints.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.MaxRecursionDepth
member.summary: Maximum recursion depth allowed, defaults to -1 (no checks).

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.MaxExecutionStackCount
member.summary: Maximum recursion stack count, defaults to -1 (as-is dotnet stacktrace).
member.remarks: Chrome and V8 based engines (ClearScript) that can handle 13955. When set to a different value except -1, it can reduce slight performance/stack trace readability drawback. (after hitting the engine's own limit), When max stack size to be exceeded, Engine throws an exception .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.RegexTimeout
member.summary: Maximum time a Regex is allowed to run, defaults to 10 seconds.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.PromiseTimeout
member.summary: Maximum time allowed for unwrapping a Promise and getting its resolved/rejected value. Defaults to 10 seconds.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.MaxArraySize
member.summary: The maximum size for JavaScript array, defaults to .

member.name: P:Jint.Options.ConstraintOptions.MaxAtomicsPauseIterations
member.summary: How many iterations is Atomics.pause allowed to instruct to wait using , defaults to 10 000.

member.name: P:Jint.Options.IntlOptions.CldrProvider
member.summary: CLDR provider for locale data. Defaults to DefaultCldrProvider which provides basic English (en-US, en-GB) support.
member.remarks: Set this to a custom ICldrProvider implementation (e.g., ICU-based provider) to enable full locale support for the Intl API.

member.name: T:Jint.Options.TemporalOptions

member.name: P:Jint.Options.TemporalOptions.TimeZoneProvider
member.summary: Time zone provider for Temporal operations. Defaults to DefaultTimeZoneProvider which uses .NET TimeZoneInfo for basic IANA time zone support.
member.remarks: Set this to a custom ITimeZoneProvider implementation (e.g., using TimeZoneConverter or NodaTime) for full IANA time zone support and better Windows compatibility.

member.name: T:Jint.ValueCoercionType

member.name: F:Jint.ValueCoercionType.None
member.summary: No coercion will be done. If there's no type converter, and error will be thrown.

member.name: F:Jint.ValueCoercionType.Boolean
member.summary: JS coercion using boolean rules "dog" == true, "" == false, 1 == true, 3 == true, 0 == false, { "prop": 1 } == true etc.

member.name: F:Jint.ValueCoercionType.Number
member.summary: JS coercion to numbers, false == 0, true == 1. valueOf functions will be used when available for object instances. Valid against targets of type: Decimal, Double, Int32, Int64.

member.name: F:Jint.ValueCoercionType.String
member.summary: JS coercion to strings, toString function will be used when available for objects.

member.name: F:Jint.ValueCoercionType.All
member.summary: All coercion rules enabled.

member.summary: Wrapping tasks to promises

member.name: F:Jint.ExperimentalFeature.All
member.summary: All coercion rules enabled.

member.param: The allowed depth. a) In case max depth is zero no recursion is allowed. b) In case max depth is equal to n it means that in one scope function can be called no more than n times.

member.summary: Disables calling 'eval' with custom code and function constructors taking function code as string. By default eval and function code parsing is allowed.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.SetWrapObjectHandler(Jint.Options,Jint.Options.WrapObjectDelegate)
member.summary: If no known type could be guessed, objects are normally wrapped as an ObjectInstance using class ObjectWrapper. This function can be used to register a handler for a customized handling.

member.summary: Sets the handler used to build stack traces. This is useful if the code currently running was transpiled (eg. TypeScript) and the source map of original code is available.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.SetTypeConverter(Jint.Options,System.Func{Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Interop.ITypeConverter})
member.summary: Sets the type converter to use.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.SetMemberAccessor(Jint.Options,Jint.Options.MemberAccessorDelegate)
member.summary: Registers a delegate that is called when CLR members are invoked. This allows to change what values are returned for specific CLR objects, or if any value is returned at all.
member.param: The delegate to invoke for each CLR member. If the delegate returns null, the standard evaluation is performed.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.AllowClr(Jint.Options,System.Reflection.Assembly[])
member.summary: Allows scripts to call CLR types directly like System.IO.File

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.CatchClrExceptions(Jint.Options)
member.summary: Exceptions thrown from CLR code are converted to JavaScript errors and can be used in at try/catch statement. By default these exceptions are bubbled to the CLR host and interrupt the script execution.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.CatchClrExceptions(Jint.Options,Jint.Options.ExceptionHandlerDelegate)
member.summary: Exceptions that thrown from CLR code are converted to JavaScript errors and can be used in at try/catch statement. By default these exceptions are bubbled to the CLR host and interrupt the script execution.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.DecorateClrExceptionErrors(Jint.Options,Jint.Options.ClrExceptionErrorDecoratorDelegate)
member.summary: Sets a decorator function that is called after a JavaScript error object is created from a CLR exception. The decorator can add custom properties, modify the error message, or enrich the error with additional context. This is only called when is enabled and the exception is caught.
member.param: A function that receives the engine, the created error object, and the original CLR exception.
member.returns: The options instance for fluent configuration.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.Configure(Jint.Options,System.Action{Jint.Engine})
