member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.EnableModules(Jint.Options,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Enables module loading in the engine via the 'require' function. By default there's no sand-boxing and you need to trust the script loading the modules not doing bad things.

member.name: M:Jint.OptionsExtensions.EnableModules(Jint.Options,Jint.Runtime.Modules.IModuleLoader)
member.summary: Enables module loading using a custom loader implementation.

member.name: P:Jint.IParsingOptions.CompileRegex
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to create compiled instances when adapting regular expressions. Defaults to , which means that in the case of non-prepared scripts and modules regular expressions will be interpreted, otherwise they will be compiled.

member.name: P:Jint.IParsingOptions.RegexTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the default timeout for created instances. Defaults to , which means that in the case of non-prepared scripts and modules the setting should apply, otherwise the default of the setting (10 seconds).
member.remarks: Please note that setting will be ignored if this option is set to a value other than .

member.name: P:Jint.IParsingOptions.Tolerant
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to parse the source code in tolerant mode. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Jint.ScriptParsingOptions.AllowReturnOutsideFunction
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to allow return statements at the top level. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Jint.ScriptParsingOptions.SourceOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the source location offset to apply when parsing. This allows mapping error locations back to the original source file when the JavaScript code is embedded within a larger file (e.g., a JSON file). The offset is 1-based for lines and 0-based for columns, matching the convention. Defaults to (no offset).

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ArgumentsInstancePool
member.summary: Cache reusable instances as we allocate them a lot.

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ConcurrentObjectPool`1
member.summary:
Generic implementation of object pooling pattern with predefined pool size limit. The main purpose is that limited number of frequently used objects can be kept in the pool for further recycling.
Notes: 1) it is not the goal to keep all returned objects. Pool is not meant for storage. If there is no space in the pool, extra returned objects will be dropped.
2) it is implied that if object was obtained from a pool, the caller will return it back in a relatively short time. Keeping checked out objects for long durations is ok, but reduces usefulness of pooling. Just new up your own.
Not returning objects to the pool in not detrimental to the pool's work, but is a bad practice. Rationale: If there is no intent for reusing the object, do not use pool - just use "new".

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ConcurrentObjectPool`1.Factory
member.remarks: Not using System.Func{T} because this file is linked into the (debugger) Formatter, which does not have that type (since it compiles against .NET 2.0).

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ConcurrentObjectPool`1.Allocate
member.summary: Produces an instance.
member.remarks: Search strategy is a simple linear probing which is chosen for it cache-friendliness. Note that Free will try to store recycled objects close to the start thus statistically reducing how far we will typically search.

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ConcurrentObjectPool`1.Free(`0)
member.summary: Returns objects to the pool.
member.remarks: Search strategy is a simple linear probing which is chosen for it cache-friendliness. Note that Free will try to store recycled objects close to the start thus statistically reducing how far we will typically search in Allocate.

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ConcurrentObjectPool`1.ForgetTrackedObject(`0,`0)
member.summary:
Removes an object from leak tracking.
This is called when an object is returned to the pool. It may also be explicitly called if an object allocated from the pool is intentionally not being returned to the pool. This can be of use with pooled arrays if the consumer wants to return a larger array to the pool than was originally allocated.

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.JsValueArrayPool
member.summary: Cache reusable array instances as we allocate them a lot.

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ObjectPool`1
member.summary:
Generic implementation of object pooling pattern with predefined pool size limit. The main purpose is that limited number of frequently used objects can be kept in the pool for further recycling.
Notes: 1) it is not the goal to keep all returned objects. Pool is not meant for storage. If there is no space in the pool, extra returned objects will be dropped.
2) it is implied that if object was obtained from a pool, the caller will return it back in a relatively short time. Keeping checked out objects for long durations is ok, but reduces usefulness of pooling. Just new up your own.
Not returning objects to the pool in not detrimental to the pool's work, but is a bad practice. Rationale: If there is no intent for reusing the object, do not use pool - just use "new".

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ObjectPool`1.Factory
member.remarks: Not using System.Func{T} because this file is linked into the (debugger) Formatter, which does not have that type (since it compiles against .NET 2.0).

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ObjectPool`1.Allocate
member.summary: Produces an instance.
member.remarks: Search strategy is a simple linear probing which is chosen for it cache-friendliness. Note that Free will try to store recycled objects close to the start thus statistically reducing how far we will typically search.

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ObjectPool`1.Free(`0)
member.summary: Returns objects to the pool.
member.remarks: Search strategy is a simple linear probing which is chosen for it cache-friendliness. Note that Free will try to store recycled objects close to the start thus statistically reducing how far we will typically search in Allocate.

member.name: M:Jint.Pooling.ObjectPool`1.ForgetTrackedObject(`0,`0)
member.summary:
Removes an object from leak tracking.
This is called when an object is returned to the pool. It may also be explicitly called if an object allocated from the pool is intentionally not being returned to the pool. This can be of use with pooled arrays if the consumer wants to return a larger array to the pool than was originally allocated.

member.name: T:Jint.Pooling.ReferencePool
member.summary: Cache reusable instances as we allocate them a lot.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakLocation
member.summary: BreakLocation is a combination of an Esprima position (line and column) and a source (path or identifier of script). Like Esprima, first column is 0 and first line is 1.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection
member.summary: Collection of breakpoints.
member.remarks: Only allows a single breakpoint at the same location (source, column and line). Adding a new breakpoint at the same location replaces the old one - this allows replacing e.g. a conditional breakpoint with a new condition (or remove the condition).

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection.Active
member.summary: Gets or sets whether breakpoints are activated. When false, all breakpoints will fail to match (and be skipped by the debugger).

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection.Set(Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPoint)
member.summary: Sets a new breakpoint. Note that this will replace any breakpoint at the same location (source/column/line).

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection.RemoveAt(Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakLocation)
member.summary: Removes breakpoint with the given location (source/column/line). Note that a null source matches any source.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection.Contains(Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakLocation)
member.summary: Checks whether collection contains a breakpoint at the given location (source/column/line). Note that a null source matches any source.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.BreakPointCollection.Clear
member.summary: Removes all breakpoints.

member.name: E:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.BeforeEvaluate
member.summary: Triggered before the engine executes/evaluates the parsed AST of a script or module.

member.name: E:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.Step
member.summary: The Step event is triggered before the engine executes a step-eligible execution point.
member.remarks: If the current step mode is , this event is never triggered. The script may still be paused by a debugger statement or breakpoint, but these will trigger the event.

member.name: E:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.Break
member.summary: The Break event is triggered when a breakpoint or debugger statement is hit.
member.remarks: This is event is not triggered if the current script location was reached by stepping. In that case, only the event is triggered.

member.name: E:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.Skip
member.summary: The Skip event is triggered for each execution point, when the point doesn't trigger a or event.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.CurrentLocation
member.summary: The location of the current (step-eligible) AST node being executed.
member.remarks: The location is available as long as DebugMode is enabled - i.e. even when not stepping or hitting a breakpoint.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.BreakPoints
member.summary: Collection of active breakpoints for the engine.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugHandler.Evaluate(Jint.Prepared{Acornima.Ast.Script}@)
member.summary: Evaluates a script (expression) within the current execution context.
member.remarks: Internally, this is used for evaluating breakpoint conditions, but may also be used for e.g. watch lists in a debugger.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugInformation.PauseType
member.summary: Indicates the type of pause that resulted in this DebugInformation being generated.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugInformation.BreakPoint
member.summary: Breakpoint at the current location. This will be set even if the pause wasn't caused by the breakpoint.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugInformation.CallStack
member.summary: The current call stack.
member.remarks: This will always include at least a call frame for the global environment.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugInformation.CurrentNode

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugInformation.Location

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope
member.summary: Scope information, bindings, and values for a single scope in the scope chain

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.ScopeType
member.summary: The type of scope. Scope types are the same as defined by Chrome devtools protocol.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.IsTopLevel
member.summary: For block scopes, indicates whether this scope is at the top level of a containing function.
member.remarks: Block scopes at the top level of a function are combined with Local scope in Chromium. This property facilitates implementing the same "flattening" in e.g. a UI. Because empty scopes are excluded in the scope chain, top level cannot be determined from the scope chain order alone.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.BindingNames
member.summary: Names of all bindings in the scope.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.BindingObject
member.summary: Binding object for ObjectEnvironmentRecords - that is, Global scope and With scope. Null for other scopes.
member.remarks: This is mainly useful as an optimization for devtools, allowing the BindingObject to be serialized directly rather than building a new transient object in response to e.g. Runtime.getProperties.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.GetBindingValue(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of a specific binding. Note that some bindings (e.g. uninitialized let/const) may return null.
member.returns: Value of the binding

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScope.SetBindingValue(System.String,Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Sets the value of an existing binding.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType
member.summary: Variable scope type. These mirror Chrome DevTools Protocol.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Global
member.summary: Global scope bindings.
member.remarks: A scope chain will only include one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Script
member.summary: Block scope bindings (let/const) defined at top level.
member.remarks: A scope chain will only include one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Local
member.summary: Function local bindings.
member.remarks: Function scoped variables. Note that variables in outer functions are in scopes. A scope chain will only include one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Block
member.summary: Block scoped bindings.
member.remarks: This scope is not used for block scoped variables (let/const) declared at the top level of the global scope. Unlike Chromium V8, it *is* used as a separate scope for block scoped variables declared at the top level of a function. A scope chain may include more than one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Catch
member.summary: Catch scope bindings.
member.remarks: This scope only includes the argument of a catch clause in a try/catch statement. A scope chain may include more than one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Closure
member.summary: Function scope bindings in outer functions.
member.remarks: Unlike Chromium V8, which will optimize variables out that aren't referenced from the inner scope, Jint includes local variables from the outer function in this scope. A scope chain may include more than one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.With
member.summary: With scope bindings.
member.remarks: Includes the bindings created from properties of object used as argument to a with statement. A scope chain may include more than one scope of this type.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Eval
member.summary: Eval scope bindings.
member.remarks: Variables declared in an evaluated string. Not implemented.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.Module
member.summary: Module scope bindings.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.DebugScopeType.WasmExpressionStack
member.summary: WebAssembly expression stack bindings.
member.remarks: Not currently implemented by Jint.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Debugger.OptionalSourceBreakLocationEqualityComparer
member.summary: Equality comparer for BreakLocation matching null Source to any other Source.
member.remarks: Equals returns true if all properties are equal - or if Source is null on either BreakLocation. GetHashCode excludes Source.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.EventLoop._isProcessing
member.summary: Tracks whether we are currently processing the event loop. Uses Interlocked.CompareExchange for atomic check-then-set to prevent TOCTOU race conditions between checking and setting. 0 = not processing, 1 = processing.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.EventLoop._waitingThreadId
member.summary: Tracks the thread ID of the thread that is currently waiting on a promise. Only this thread (or any thread if -1) is allowed to process continuations. This prevents background threads (e.g., Task.ContinueWith callbacks) from executing JavaScript code on the Engine.

member.name: F:Jint.Runtime.EventLoop._eventAvailable
member.summary: Async wake signal used by to release the thread while waiting for events. Set by when new work arrives. Uses TaskCompletionSource<bool> for compatibility with netstandard2.0/net462.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.EventLoop.WaitForEventAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Waits asynchronously for events to be enqueued, releasing the current thread. Used by the async API path (EvaluateAsync/ExecuteAsync/InvokeAsync) to avoid blocking a thread during IO-bound operations. During the wait, zero threads are consumed.
member.returns: A task that completes when events are available or cancellation is requested.

member.summary: Method's match score tells how far away it's from ideal candidate. 0 = ideal, bigger the the number, the farther away the candidate is from ideal match. Negative signals impossible match.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IObjectConverter
member.summary: When implemented, converts a CLR value to a instance

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IReferenceResolver
member.summary: Reference resolver allows customizing behavior for reference resolving. This can be useful in cases where you want to ignore long chain of property accesses that might throw if anything is null or undefined. An example of such is var a = obj.field.subField.value. Custom resolver could accept chain to return null/undefined on first occurrence.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IReferenceResolver.TryUnresolvableReference(Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Reference,Jint.Native.JsValue@)
member.summary: When unresolvable reference occurs, check if another value can be provided instead of it.
member.remarks: A reference error will be thrown if this method return false.
member.param: The reference that is being processed.
Value that should be used instead of undefined.
member.returns: Whether to use instead of undefined.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IReferenceResolver.TryPropertyReference(Jint.Engine,Jint.Runtime.Reference,Jint.Native.JsValue@)
member.summary: When property reference is being processed, resolve to other value if needed.
member.param: The reference that is being processed.
Value that should be used instead of reference target.
member.returns: Whether to use instead of reference's value.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IReferenceResolver.TryGetCallable(Jint.Engine,System.Object,Jint.Native.JsValue@)
member.summary: When evaluating a function call and a target that is not an object is encountered, custom implementation can return a value to call.
member.remarks: A reference error will be thrown if this method return false.
member.param: Value that should be used when this method return true. Should be .
member.returns: Whether to use instead of undefined.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interop.IReferenceResolver.CheckCoercible(Jint.Native.JsValue)
member.summary: Check whether objects property value is valid.
member.returns: Whether to accept the value.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.Interpreter.JintFunctionDefinition.EnvironmentEscapeAstVisitor
member.summary: Checks if a function's per-call environment may escape (be captured by closures). If true, the environment cannot be pooled/cached for reuse.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interpreter.JintFunctionDefinition.EnvironmentEscapeAstVisitor.MayEscapeWithReferences(Acornima.Ast.IFunction,Jint.Key[])
member.summary: Smarter escape analysis: checks if any closures in the function body actually reference any of the specified slot variable names. If closures exist but don't reference any slot variables, the environment can still be safely cached.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interpreter.JintFunctionDefinition.EnvironmentEscapeAstVisitor.ScanForCapturingReferences(Acornima.Ast.Node,Jint.Key[])
member.summary: Scans the node tree for closures that reference any of the specified slot names. When a closure is found, its body is searched for identifier references matching slot names. eval() and with statements always cause escape (dynamic references can't be analyzed).

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.Interpreter.JintFunctionDefinition.EnvironmentEscapeAstVisitor.ClosureReferencesAny(Acornima.Ast.Node,Jint.Key[])
member.summary: Checks if a closure node (function/arrow/class) references any of the specified names. Walks the entire closure tree looking for matching identifiers, including nested functions (since they can access outer variables through the scope chain).

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable
member.summary: Interface for entities that can suspend execution (generators, async functions). Aligns with TC39 execution context suspension semantics.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.IsSuspended
member.summary: Whether this suspendable is currently in a suspended state. For generators: SuspendedStart or SuspendedYield For async functions: SuspendedAwait

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.IsResuming
member.summary: Whether we are resuming from a suspended state.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.SuspendedValue
member.summary: The value yielded/awaited when suspended. For generators: the yielded value For async functions: the resume value from awaited promise

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.LastSuspensionNode
member.summary: The AST node where execution last suspended (yield or await expression). Unified property for tracking suspension location across all suspendable types.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.ReturnRequested
member.summary: Signals that an early return was requested (e.g., generator.return() was called). For generators: true when generator.return() was called For async functions/generators: typically false

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.PendingCompletionType
member.summary: Tracks the pending completion type when suspended in a finally block.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.PendingCompletionValue
member.summary: Tracks the pending completion value when suspended in a finally block.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.CurrentFinallyStatement
member.summary: The try statement whose finally block we're currently executing. Used to properly resume execution in finally blocks.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ISuspendable.Data
member.summary: Dictionary for tracking state of loops, destructuring, etc. during async execution. Keyed by Jint expression/statement instances (not AST nodes) to avoid collisions.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.ITimeSystem
member.summary: Date related operations that can replaced with implementation that can handle also full IANA data as recommended by the JS spec. Jint comes with which is based on built-in data which might be incomplete.
member.remarks: This interface intentionally uses long instead of DateTime/DateTimeOffset as DateTime/DateTimeOffset cannot handle neither negative years nor the date range that JS can.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.ITimeSystem.GetUtcNow
member.summary: Retrieves current UTC time.
member.returns: Current UTC time.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ITimeSystem.DefaultTimeZone
member.summary: Return the default time zone system is using. Usually , but can be altered via engine configuration, see .

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.ITimeSystem.TryParse(System.String,System.Int64@)
member.summary: Tries to parse given time presentation string as JS date presentation based on epoch.
member.param: The milliseconds since the UNIX epoch, can be negative for values before 1970.
member.returns: true, if succeeded.

member.name: M:Jint.Runtime.ITimeSystem.GetUtcOffset(System.Int64)
member.summary: Retrieves UTC offset for given date presented as milliseconds since the Unix epoch. Defaults to using using the configured time zone.
member.param: Date as milliseconds since the Unix epoch, may be negative (for instants before the epoch).

The type of the dictionary values.
member.remarks: This dictionary makes it possible to get the index of a key and a key based on an index. It can be costly to find the index of a key because it must be searched for linearly. It can be costly to insert a key/value pair because other key's indexes must be adjusted. It can be costly to remove a key/value pair because other keys' indexes must be adjusted.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.SuspendData
member.summary: Base class for generator suspension state. Used to track iterator state when a generator yields inside constructs like for-of loops or destructuring patterns.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.SuspendData.Iterator
member.summary: The iterator instance that was in progress when the generator suspended. Null for constructs that don't use iterators (e.g., regular for loops).

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.DestructuringSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of an array destructuring pattern when a generator yields inside it. When a generator yields during array destructuring (e.g., [x[yield]] = iterable), the iterator must be preserved so it can be properly closed when the generator completes or returns.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.DestructuringSuspendData.Done
member.summary: Whether the iterator has been exhausted (done=true).

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.ForOfSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of a for-of/for-in loop when a generator yields inside it.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForOfSuspendData.CurrentValue
member.summary: The current value being processed (from TryIteratorStep). Needed when yield happens during destructuring or body execution.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForOfSuspendData.AccumulatedValue
member.summary: The accumulated result value (v) from previous iterations.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForOfSuspendData.IterationEnv
member.summary: The iteration environment for lexical bindings (let/const in for-of).

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.ForLoopSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of a regular for loop when a generator yields inside it. Saves loop variable values so they can be restored when resuming.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForLoopSuspendData.BoundValues
member.summary: The saved values of loop variables (let bindings in for loop init).

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.ForAwaitSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of a for-await-of loop when an async function awaits inside it.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForAwaitSuspendData.ResolvedIteratorResult
member.summary: The resolved iterator result from awaiting the next() Promise.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.ForAwaitSuspendData.AccumulatedValue
member.summary: The accumulated result value (v) from previous iterations.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.BlockSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of a block with lexical bindings when execution suspends within it.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.BlockSuspendData.BlockEnvironment
member.summary: The block environment to reuse when resuming.

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.BlockSuspendData.OuterEnvironment
member.summary: The outer environment to restore after the block completes.

member.name: T:Jint.Runtime.SequenceSuspendData
member.summary: Stores the state of a sequence expression when a generator yields inside it. Tracks which sub-expression was being evaluated when the generator suspended, so on resume we skip already-evaluated sub-expressions (avoiding duplicate side effects).

member.name: P:Jint.Runtime.SequenceSuspendData.ExpressionIndex
member.summary: The index of the sub-expression that was being evaluated when the generator suspended.

member.name: T:System.Threading.Lock
member.summary: Provides a way to get mutual exclusion in regions of code between different threads. A lock may be held by one thread at a time.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Enter
member.summary: Enters the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock without waiting. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.
member.returns: true if the lock was entered, false otherwise.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.
member.param: The rough duration for which the method will wait if the lock is not available. The timeout is converted to a number of milliseconds by casting of the timeout to an integer value. A value representing 0 milliseconds specifies that the method should not wait, and a value representing or -1 milliseconds specifies that the method should wait indefinitely until the lock is entered.
member.returns: true if the lock was entered, false otherwise.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.TryEnter(System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to enter the lock, waiting for roughly the specified duration. If the lock is entered, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock.
member.param: The rough duration in milliseconds for which the method will wait if the lock is not available. A value of 0 specifies that the method should not wait, and a value of or -1 specifies that the method should wait indefinitely until the lock is entered.
member.returns: true if the lock was entered, false otherwise.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.Exit
member.summary: Exits the lock.

member.name: M:System.Threading.Lock.EnterScope
member.summary: Enters the lock and returns a that may be disposed to exit the lock. Once the method returns, the calling thread would be the only thread that holds the lock. This method is intended to be used along with a language construct that would automatically dispose the , such as with the C# using statement.
member.returns: A that may be disposed to exit the lock.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The priority of the attributed member. Higher numbers are prioritized, lower numbers are deprioritized. 0 is the default if no attribute is present.

member.summary: The priority of the member.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ParamCollectionAttribute

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DisableRuntimeMarshallingAttribute
member.summary: Disables the built-in runtime managed/unmanaged marshalling subsystem for P/Invokes, Delegate types, and unmanaged function pointer invocations.
member.remarks:
The built-in marshalling subsystem has some behaviors that cannot be changed due to backward-compatibility requirements. This attribute allows disabling the built-in subsystem and instead uses the following rules for P/Invokes, Delegates, and unmanaged function pointer invocations:
- All value types that do not contain reference type fields recursively (unmanaged in C#) are blittable - Value types that recursively have any fields that have [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Auto)] are disallowed from interop. - All reference types are disallowed from usage in interop scenarios. - SetLastError support in P/Invokes is disabled. - varargs support is disabled. - LCIDConversionAttribute support is disabled.

member.remarks:
Callers can apply a or use guards to prevent calls to APIs on unsupported operating systems.
A given platform should only be specified once.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates a custom guard field, property or method with a supported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple supported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements in order to safely call platform specific APIs.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformAttribute

member.summary: Annotates the custom guard field, property or method with an unsupported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple unsupported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements as a guard to safely call APIs unsupported on those platforms.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.SuppressGCTransitionAttribute
member.summary: An attribute used to indicate a GC transition should be skipped when making an unmanaged function call.
member.remarks:
This attribute is ignored if applied to a method without the .
Forgoing this transition can yield benefits when the cost of the transition is more than the execution time of the unmanaged function. However, avoiding this transition removes some of the guarantees the runtime provides through a normal P/Invoke. When exiting the managed runtime to enter an unmanaged function the GC must transition from Cooperative mode into Preemptive mode. Full details on these modes can be found at https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/coding-guidelines/clr-code-guide.md#2.1.8. Suppressing the GC transition is an advanced scenario and should not be done without fully understanding potential consequences.
One of these consequences is an impact to Mixed-mode debugging (https://docs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/debugger/how-to-debug-in-mixed-mode). During Mixed-mode debugging, it is not possible to step into or set breakpoints in a P/Invoke that has been marked with this attribute. A workaround is to switch to native debugging and set a breakpoint in the native function. In general, usage of this attribute is not recommended if debugging the P/Invoke is important, for example stepping through the native code or diagnosing an exception thrown from the native code.
The runtime may load the native library for method marked with this attribute in advance before the method is called for the first time. Usage of this attribute is not recommended for platform neutral libraries with conditional platform specific code.
The P/Invoke method that this attribute is applied to must have all of the following properties: * Native function always executes for a trivial amount of time (less than 1 microsecond). * Native function does not perform a blocking syscall (e.g. any type of I/O). * Native function does not call back into the runtime (e.g. Reverse P/Invoke). * Native function does not throw exceptions. * Native function does not manipulate locks or other concurrency primitives.
Consequences of invalid uses of this attribute: * GC starvation. * Immediate runtime termination. * Data corruption.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute
member.summary: Any method marked with can be directly called from native code. The function token can be loaded to a local variable using the address-of operator in C# and passed as a callback to a native method.
member.remarks: Methods marked with this attribute have the following restrictions: * Method must be marked "static". * Must not be called from managed code. * Must only have blittable arguments.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.CallConvs

member.remarks: Supplied types must be from the official "System.Runtime.CompilerServices" namespace and be of the form "CallConvXXX".

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.EntryPoint

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.summary: States a dependency that one member has on another.
member.remarks: This can be used to inform tooling of a dependency that is otherwise not evident purely from metadata and IL, for example a member relied on via reflection.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified signature of a member on the same type as the consumer.
member.param: The signature of the member depended on.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureGuardAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified public static boolean get-only property guards access to the specified feature.
member.remarks: Analyzers can use this to prevent warnings on calls to code that is annotated as requiring that feature, when the callsite is guarded by a call to the property.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureGuardAttribute.#ctor(System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified feature type.
member.param: The type that represents the feature guarded by the property.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureGuardAttribute.FeatureType
member.summary: The type that represents the feature guarded by the property.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureSwitchDefinitionAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified public static boolean get-only property corresponds to the feature switch specified by name.
member.remarks: IL rewriters and compilers can use this to substitute the return value of the specified property with the value of the feature switch.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureSwitchDefinitionAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified feature switch name.
member.param: The name of the feature switch that provides the value for the specified property.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.FeatureSwitchDefinitionAttribute.SwitchName
member.summary: The name of the feature switch that provides the value for the specified property.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified member requires assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: A message that contains information about the need for assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets an optional message that contains information about the need for assembly files to be on disk.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFilesAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the member, why it requires assembly files to be on disk, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.DebuggerDisableUserUnhandledExceptionsAttribute
member.summary: If a .NET Debugger is attached which supports the Debugger.BreakForUserUnhandledException(Exception) API, this attribute will prevent the debugger from breaking on user-unhandled exceptions when the exception is caught by a method with this attribute, unless BreakForUserUnhandledException is called.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.StackTraceHiddenAttribute
member.summary: Types and Methods attributed with StackTraceHidden will be omitted from the stack trace text shown in StackTrace.ToString() and Exception.StackTrace

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.StackTraceHiddenAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
